conjecture_title: Open case H = K3 for Conjecture 1.4
paper_title: Sparse graphs with no polynomial-sized anticomplete pairs
summary: The $K_3$ (triangle) case of Conjecture 1.4 from arXiv:1810.00058 remains open as of May 2026. The paper's main result covers all almost-bipartite $H$, but since $K_3$ contains a triangle it lies outside this class. Substantial follow-up work by Buci\'c, Fox, Nguyen, Pham, Scott, and Seymour has made progress on related conjectures in the same paper (the polynomial R\"odl / Fox-Sudakov conjecture, Conjecture 1.7) and on the Erd\H{o}s-Hajnal programme more broadly, but no paper specifically resolving the $K_3$ case of Conjecture 1.4 was found.

internal_refs_verified.note: Abstract-level fetch could not confirm citation of 1810.00058 or discussion of K_3 case of Conjecture 1.4; the contributions listed in the corpus concern Theorem 1.3 (Erd\H{o}s-Hajnal loglog bound) and appear unrelated to Conjecture 1.4 specifically.

Abstract-level fetch confirmed this paper proves equivalence of the polynomial R\"odl conjecture and Erd\H{o}s-Hajnal conjecture; could not confirm it cites 1810.00058 or addresses Conjecture 1.4 for K_3. The result concerns Conjecture 1.7 of 1810.00058 (polynomial R\"odl) rather than Conjecture 1.4 (anticomplete pairs).
Not fetched within call budget. Corpus contribution references Conjecture 1.7 (via the Buci\'c-Fox-Pham equivalence), not Conjecture 1.4 for K_3.

HTML fetch confirmed this paper (Fox, Nguyen, Scott, Seymour) proves the Fox-Sudakov conjecture for H=P_4 and vertex-substitution variants. The paper does not address Conjecture 1.4 for K_3; its main conjecture (Conjecture 1.2 / Fox-Sudakov) is a different conjecture in the same family.

notes: The internal references from the curated corpus appear to concern Conjecture 1.7 (polynomial R\"odl / Fox-Sudakov conjecture) from the same paper, not Conjecture 1.4 about polynomial-sized anticomplete pairs. The equivalence result of Buci\'c-Fox-Pham (2403.08303) and the Erd\H{o}s-Hajnal loglog improvement (2301.10147) are part of the same broad programme but do not directly settle Conjecture 1.4 for K_3. The K_3 case of Conjecture 1.4 is noted as still open in the 2020 paper and no follow-up resolving it was found in the indexed literature.
